summary: Centralized missing-tool diagnostics in the Just helper so Linux keeps Nix guidance while macOS receives native bootstrap or PATH guidance, and routed coverage, mutation, and security tool checks through that helper.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The generic just missing-tool helper emits platform-aware guidance instead of always saying to run inside Nix.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: justfile now branches on `uname -s`; evidence/m4-platform-aware-tool-missing-diagnostics/require-tool-helper-snippet.log records the helper body.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Linux diagnostics continue to point required repo hygiene and security tools at nix develop.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-platform-aware-tool-missing-diagnostics/missing-tool-smoke-linux.log records `required tool missing: madobe-definitely-missing-tool; run inside nix develop` with exit 127.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: macOS diagnostics do not suggest installing or using Nix and direct users toward just macos-bootstrap or native PATH setup.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: The Darwin branch in evidence/m4-platform-aware-tool-missing-diagnostics/require-tool-helper-snippet.log emits native macOS guidance, and the helper uses `@` to prevent echoing the Linux fallback branch.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: A targeted smoke test records the Linux message for a definitely missing tool.
just fmt and just check pass on Linux.
Evidence records validation and qd reports.
No product runtime, capture, encode, transport, decode, render, presentation, cross-device, or latency claim is made.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: Only justfile diagnostics, qd export metadata, evidence, and reports changed.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node changes static Just diagnostics only and requires no external hardware or cross-device validation.
